narrative.headline: 2020 Dodge Durango: exterior lighting leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 53 complaints with NHTSA. Exterior lighting is the most reported area at 38%, followed by the electrical system (15%) and engine (13%). No deaths are reported. Two of the electrical-system reports involved a fire, and the exterior-lighting and structure reports together account for 6 injuries, so both areas are worth a close look.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: The biggest complaint area: 20 reports (38%), tied to 3 injuries. Owners describe a sharp edge on the rear taillight where it meets the lift gate.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 8 reports (15%), including 2 that involved a fire. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.sample: The start stop system is the Durango is terrible! That was my first problem. My second issue is, out of nowhere. We now have inner engine issues. The engine will not hold compression, but there are no leaks! I have only had a vehicle for six months! Now I am paying on a vehicle.
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Complaints mention the start-stop system and engine trouble.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 4 reports (8%), linked to 3 injuries. Like the lighting complaints, these describe a sharp edge near the rear taillight and lift gate.

issues.sample: Drivers seatbelt buckle does not register, resulting in active chiming of the unbuckled seat belt alarm and disengaging of the airbag system.  Driver must hold the seatbelt with downward pressure continuously in order to register the seat belt and return the vehicle to normal ope
issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: The fewest at 3 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Complaints mention the driver seatbelt buckle and airbag system.
